news_items.title: Spain vs Uruguay at World Cup 2026: match prediction, team news and lineups | World Cup 2026 News | Al Jazeera
news_items.author: Alexander Hoad
news_items.text:
Spain vs Uruguay at World Cup 2026: match prediction, team news and lineups | World Cup 2026 News | Al Jazeera

Listen (6 mins)

Save

Share

Share on Facebook Share on Twitter Share on Whatsapp Copy link

Add Al Jazeera on Google

Mikel Oyarzabal and Lamine Yamal combined for three goals against Saudi Arabia [Claudia Greco/Reuters]

Who: Spain vs Uruguay What: FIFA World Cup 2026 Group H match Where: Guadalajara Stadium, Mexico When: Friday, 6pm local time (00:00 GMT) How to follow: We’ll have all the buildup on Al Jazeera Sport from 20:30 GMT ahead of our live text commentary stream.

The only clash of former FIFA World Cup winners in the group stage of this edition comes in the final round of games, and it’s a crucial one for both Uruguay and Spain in Group H.

## Recommended Stories

- Who’s being left out of the World Cup?
- US says Iran can stay for two days before Egypt World Cup game
- ‘Together all the time’: Trump, Infantino to present World Cup final trophy
- World Cup knockouts: Who has made it to the round of 32 stage?

Spain sit top of the table with four points and know a win will seal first place, but though Uruguay are second, they need at least a point to be sure

news_items.title: Preview: Uruguay vs Spain - prediction, team news, lineups - Sports Mole
news_items.text:
Preview: Uruguay vs Spain - prediction, team news, lineups - Sports Mole

Menu

World Cup Gameweek 3

Uruguay

Guadalajara Stadium

Spain

# Preview: Uruguay vs Spain - prediction, team news, lineups

By Matt Law| 24 Jun 2026 19:35, Last updated: 25 Jun 2026 11:14

© Iconsport / Xinhua

Both aiming to book their spot in the knockout round of the 2026 World Cup, Spain and Uruguay will lock horns in Group H on Friday.

Spain are currently top of the section, picking up four points from two matches, while Uruguay are second, collecting two points from their two games at the tournament.

---

## Match preview

Uruguay vs. Spain World Cup 2026 Match Preview

Spain opened their 2026 World Cup with an underwhelming goalless draw against Cape Verde, but La Roja were excellent in their clash against Saudi Arabia last time out, running out 4-0 winners, with Mikel Oyarzabal(two) and Lamine Yamal on the scoresheet.

Luis de la Fuente's team are currently top of Group H on four points, two points ahead of second-placed Uruguay and third-placed Cape Verde ahead of the final matchday.

Spain will qualify for the knockout round of the 2026 World Cup as Group H winners should they beat Uruguay, whi

news_items.title: Uruguay lineup vs. Spain: Predicted XI for 2026 World Cup fixture including double injury blow - Sports Mole
news_items.text:
Uruguay lineup vs. Spain: Predicted XI for 2026 World Cup fixture including double injury blow - Sports Mole

Menu

World Cup Gameweek 3

Uruguay

Guadalajara Stadium

Spain

# Uruguay lineup vs. Spain: Predicted XI for 2026 World Cup fixture including double injury blow

By Matt Law| 24 Jun 2026 19:49, Last updated: 24 Jun 2026 19:52

© Iconsport / PA Images

Uruguay will be without the services of Ronald Araujo(calf) and Giorgian de Arrascaeta (calf) for Friday's 2026 World Cup fixture against Spain.

The South American nation had been hopeful that both players would be able to play some part in the group stage of this summer's competition.

However, Uruguay head coach Marcelo Bielsa has ruled both Araujo and De Arrascaeta out of the contest, which will determine the national team's fate at the tournament.

There could be a key change in the final third of the field for this match, with Darwin Nunez potentially being introduced in place of Federico Vinas.

Nunez has 13 goals for his country and could be given the nod due to Vinas' struggles in the 2-2 draw with Cape Verde last time out.

Agustin Canobbio and Maxi Araujo were both in impressive form against Cape Verde and are set

# Preview: Uruguay vs Spain - prediction, team news, lineups

By Matt Law| 24 Jun 2026 19:35, Last updated: 25 Jun 2026 11:14

© Iconsport / Xinhua

Both aiming to book their spot in the knockout round of the 2026 World Cup, Spain and Uruguay will lock horns in Group H on Friday.

Spain are currently top of the section, picking up four points from two matches, while Uruguay are second, collecting two points from their two games at the tournament.

---

## Match preview

Uruguay vs. Spain World Cup 2026 Match Preview

Spain opened their 2026 World Cup with an underwhelming goalless draw against Cape Verde, but La Roja were excellent in their clash against Saudi Arabia last time out, running out 4-0 winners, with Mikel Oyarzabal(two) and Lamine Yamal on the scoresheet.

Luis de la Fuente's team are currently top of Group H on four points, two points ahead of second-placed Uruguay and third-placed Cape Verde ahead of the final matchday.

Spain will qualify for the knockout round of the 2026 World Cup as Group H winners should they beat Uruguay, whi"}

# Uruguay lineup vs. Spain: Predicted XI for 2026 World Cup fixture including double injury blow

By Matt Law| 24 Jun 2026 19:49, Last updated: 24 Jun 2026 19:52

© Iconsport / PA Images

Uruguay will be without the services of Ronald Araujo(calf) and Giorgian de Arrascaeta (calf) for Friday's 2026 World Cup fixture against Spain.

The South American nation had been hopeful that both players would be able to play some part in the group stage of this summer's competition.

However, Uruguay head coach Marcelo Bielsa has ruled both Araujo and De Arrascaeta out of the contest, which will determine the national team's fate at the tournament.

There could be a key change in the final third of the field for this match, with Darwin Nunez potentially being introduced in place of Federico Vinas.

Nunez has 13 goals for his country and could be given the nod due to Vinas' struggles in the 2-2 draw with Cape Verde last time out.

Agustin Canobbio and Maxi Araujo were both in impressive form against Cape Verde and are set "}
